subject: GR-API flow, VF Module, SDNC GR API Fixes
commit_message:
GR-API flow, VF Module, SDNC GR API Fixes

Fixes the new GR-API flow 
Various fixes in the create VF Module area
Fixes issues parsing the SDNC GR API response
Fixes new resource type in ASDC controller 
Fixes AppC mismatch in JSON marshaling

Issue-ID: SO-821
Change-Id: I4ace0d348483ab1303fcfee26c5801674be51466
Signed-off-by: Benjamin, Max (mb388a) <mb388a@us.att.com>

Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

Hi Max, can you provide details in the summary portion of your commit message (two lines below title)?

If we have any issue in the future, it would be nice to know what changes this introduced by reading the commit message.

Also, As a committer and reviewer I need to be able to look at what the code is supposed to do and what the code actually does.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

This pull contains fixes to the new GR-API flow that i have been demonstrating on t he SO calls.  The majority of the fixes are around the create VF Module area.  We had some issues parsing the SDNC GR API response.  The other fixes are around a new resource type in ASDC controller that needed updates.  The updates to AppC just fixes some mis match in the JSON marshalling.  If we need more details than this please let me know
Patch Set 2: Commit message was updated.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+2

Code review is one of the biggest strengths of Open Source, but it cannot be done if we don't conform to some basic ground rules. It's hard to appropriately review a patch that doesn't contain adequate detail in the commit message. It's hard to appropriately review a patch that does not conform it's subject to a single coherent area. It's hard to appropriately review a patch that is very large.

I know in ONAP these types of patches have been pushed through regardless in the past. I can't imagine that appropriate code review happened in the community on those patches. I'm hoping that we will collectively learn from longstanding and very successful open source projects and curtail patches that are not easy to review, or that are not review-able in the future. 

That said. I've reviewed this with the information provided by Steve. It seems fine.
